title: dendrocnide stenophylla
intro: Dendrocnide stenophylla, also known as the stinging tree, is a species of flowering plant in the nettle family Urticaceae, native to Australia. The plant contains urticating hairs that cause severe skin irritation and pain upon contact. Traditionally, the stinging tree has been used in Australian Aboriginal medicine to treat various ailments, including pain, fever, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Dendrocnide stenophylla are typically made from the plant's leaves and stems, and can be used topically or internally, although caution is advised due to the plant's potent and potentially toxic effects.
